story: I went to high school in the 1970s and the 8-track of this album was always playing in my first car.

album: The Best of Southside Johnny & the Asbury Jukes
story: My high school sweetheart spent a summer in New York City
column7: and brought me back this CD as a gift. I was stuck in my own music rut and this jolted me out of it. I was obscessed with this CD and listened to it constantly. It always reminds me of her. It was over 40 years before I finally saw this artist live.

story: In college I entered my jazz years. David Sanborn was a staple that I listened to quite often
column10: and others of that era. It reminds me a lot of that time in my life.

story: I got married in 1998 and this was a song my wife liked. It wasn't the sort of song I normally listened to but it opened my musical horions and it reminds me of that time in my life.
Queen was a staple of rock in the 1970s and 1980s. This song always makes me grin from its cheekiness. I played it in the car a lot while my kids were growing up. They became Queen fans
column7: and we finally got to see Queen + Adam Lambert live in Chicago. Another great memory of my kids.

story: My son was a fan of Muse when this song came out. He had already seen them live a couple of times. He
column8: and I saw them live on this tour
column9: and it always reminds me of the joy those two people bring to my life

story: I know one of the members of the band. This reminds me of the positive experience of moving to Virginia shortly after my heart surgery.
The song was released way before 2025
column7: but it was 2025 when I heard this band for the first time. I've been hooked on them ever since. While this song initially seems like a song about drinking
column8: it's really about spending time with and recognizing good friends and the impermance of life.
